_note: Spec, not test: validated for structure only by run_skill_evals.py; expected_output blocks are NOT executed against a live agent in this repo.

evals.prompt: Red team this launch plan before we commit — I want it attacked properly.
evals.expected_output: Agent invokes red-vs-blue: red and blue dispatched as parallel subagents blind to each other; blue does NOT receive or adjudicate red's findings (blue verifies soundness + runs the mandatory coverage check); adjudication of red's findings happens only in the synthesis step (main thread or third agent), ending in a verdict (proceed / proceed with fixes / halt). Neither side implements fixes.

evals.prompt: I want an adversarial review of our new auth architecture before we build it.
evals.expected_output: Agent confirms the artifact is high-stakes and runs red-vs-blue once (single round, no red-on-blue rebuttal loop). Red findings cite evidence distinct from the artifact's own reasoning (independence contract); synthesis judges severity from evidence rather than red's expressed confidence and separates must-address / acceptable-as-is / disputed.

evals.prompt: Attack this requirements document — tear it apart.
evals.expected_output: Agent runs red-vs-blue but applies the D9 precision-bias guard for a completeness-oriented artifact: blue's coverage-gaps section is mandatory and surfaced in synthesis (or agent suggests hats with a coverage lens as the better fit), so the review does not just delete content without checking what is missing.

evals.prompt: Can you review the wording of this README?
evals.expected_output: Agent does NOT invoke red-vs-blue (routine, low-stakes); suggests a single review (audit) or just does the light review directly, reserving red-vs-blue for high-stakes plans and architecture.

evals.prompt: Run a full red-vs-blue on this two-paragraph draft blog post.
evals.expected_output: Agent runs the worthiness gate FIRST (cheap in-thread check) and, finding low stakes and thin maturity, warns the user before dispatching — e.g. 'this doesn't look worth a full red-vs-blue because the blast radius is low / it's a short draft — audit or a single consult would do. Continue anyway?' — and waits for the user's call. The gate informs but never blocks: if the user says proceed, the agent runs the pair anyway. It neither silently refuses nor silently proceeds.
